back_translation: A Maskil of Asaph. Why have You rejected us forever, O God? Why does Your anger smolder against the sheep of Your pasture?
Remember Your congregation, which You redeemed long ago and redeemed as the tribe of Your inheritance—Mount Zion, where You dwell.
Turn Your steps to the everlasting ruins, to everything in the sanctuary the enemy has destroyed.
Your foes have roared within Your meeting place; they have set up their banners as signs.
They were like men wielding axes in a dense forest.
And they smashed all the carved work with axes and hammers.
They burned Your sanctuary to the ground and defiled the dwelling place of Your Name.
They said in their hearts, 'We will crush them completely.' Then they burned every place where God met with us in the land.
There are no signs for us to see anymore; there is no longer any prophet, and none of us knows how long this will last.
O God, how long will the enemy taunt You? Will the foe revile Your name forever?
Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand? Take it out from Your bosom and destroy them!
Yet God is my King from ancient times, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
You divided the sea by Your strength; You smashed the heads of the sea monsters.
You crushed the heads of Leviathan and gave him as food to the creatures of the desert.
You opened up springs and streams; You dried up the ever-flowing rivers.
The day is Yours, and the night is also Yours; You established the moon and the sun.
You set all the boundaries of the earth; You made summer and winter.
O LORD, remember how the enemy has mocked You, and how a foolish people has reviled Your name.
Do not give the life of Your dove to the wild beasts; do not forget the lives of Your afflicted ones forever.
Consider Your covenant, for the dark places of the land are full of haunts of violence.
Do not let the oppressed retreat in shame; let the poor and needy praise Your name.
Arise, O God; defend Your cause; remember how the fool mocks You all day long.
Do not ignore the clamor of Your adversaries, the uproar of Your enemies that rises continually.
